title: Increment should be avoided to set boolean variables to 'true'
description: Increment should not be used to set boolean variables to 'true'Analyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellobsoleteconfusingWhy is this an issue?More InfoIt is possible to use the increment operator++, to set the value of abool(C++) or_Bool(C) variable totrue. But this feature has been deprecated in C++ since the 1998 version of the standard, removed in C++17, and even where allowed, is simply confusing.Noncompliant code examplebool alive; ... alive++;Compliant solutionbool alive; ..
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Increment operator should not be used to set boolean variables to 'true'. Use direct assignment (var = true).

conditions.message: Boolean variable '{captured}' should be set to true using direct assignment, not increment operator.
